simplified_promise: Build 1.5 million new homes within the 2024_2029 parliament.
Restore mandatory local housing targets by updating the NPPF immediately after taking office.
Require all planning authorities to maintain up-to-date Local Plans; strengthen the presumption in favour of development.
Fund additional planning officers in local authorities, financed through a higher stamp duty surcharge on non-UK resident buyers.
Introduce a brownfield-first planning approach and fast-track approvals for urban brownfield sites.
Introduce 'grey belt' land category within the Green Belt and apply 'golden rules' to ensure community and environmental benefit from any release.
Deliver the largest increase in social and affordable housing in a generation by strengthening planning obligations and reforming the Affordable Homes Programme.
Prioritise new social rented homes and review Right to Buy discounts to protect social housing stock.
Give first-time buyers priority access to new housing developments and restrict bulk sales of new homes to international investors.
Launch a permanent mortgage guarantee scheme for first-time buyers with small deposits.
Abolish Section 21 no-fault evictions and extend Awaab's Law to the private rented sector.
Give renters a legal right to challenge unreasonable rent increases.
Enact Law Commission leasehold reforms; ban new leasehold flats and make commonhold the default tenure.
Cap or ban unaffordable ground rents and end unfair 'fleecehold' maintenance charges on private estates.
Develop a cross-government homelessness strategy with Mayors and Councils to end homelessness.
Accelerate cladding remediation and ensure building safety costs fall on developers, not leaseholders.
Require Combined and Mayoral Authorities to produce strategic housing growth plans and give them new planning powers.
Reform compulsory purchase rules so landowners receive fair value, not inflated hope value, enabling faster land assembly for housing.
